121081.explanation: Pro Olst-Wijhe believes residents should have influence on important decisions. Therefore, we make decisions with residents, not about them. A referendum can help with this. It is important to be clear about the extent of citizens’ influence: consultation or co-decision.

121085.explanation: The transition to sustainable energy is important for the future of our children. This reduces dependence on expensive energy from the world market. The municipality assists at the start so that the revenues return to the community and municipality rather than only to investors.

121088.explanation: Housing seasonal workers requires careful choices. Mandatory housing on their own property is not always suitable or desirable. The municipality must work with employers to find good solutions that fit the environment and livability of villages.

121091.explanation: Terrace heating does not fit with a sustainable municipality. It consumes a lot of energy and yields little. Blankets are a good alternative. By banning terrace heating, we save energy and take a clear step towards a more sustainable living environment.

121092.explanation: A fireworks display is not a task for the municipality. It costs a lot of money and organization and mainly benefits one village. We prefer to spend that money on things that contribute to safety, livability, and amenities throughout the year.

121099.explanation: Pro Olst-Wijhe wants to make music lessons possible for all children at school. This helps children develop and stimulates creativity.

121100.explanation: Sports clubs are important for social contact and health. The municipality already provides subsidies to all sports clubs, but it is more important that the municipality supports them in word and deed. This way, we ensure sports remain accessible to everyone and clubs keep their place in the community.

121103.explanation: Additional enforcement officers can help prevent nuisance and ensure a pleasant living environment in the villages. Rules only have value with proper enforcement.

121104.explanation: Camera surveillance is not always the best solution. It mainly creates an illusion of safety. Real safety mainly requires presence and contact. Pro Olst-Wijhe prefers to invest the money for cameras there.

121108.explanation: The housing shortage calls for space, not unnecessary restrictions. Therefore, Pro Olst-Wijhe is open to this solution in the short term. But in the long term, we prefer to focus on real, affordable homes suitable for permanent residence.

121109.explanation: The housing crisis calls for space, not blockades. Building within and directly adjacent to the villages is strongly preferred, but we do not rule out expansion if this is necessary to quickly realize enough affordable housing.

121110.explanation: Not everyone can work, but everyone must be able to participate. Mandatory volunteer work is not always appropriate. We focus on trust, guidance, and training where it fits someone's situation. The goal is to help people out of the social assistance net, not to trap them in it.

121111.explanation: Debt often leads to more debt. Debt forgiveness helps prevent deterioration of problems and debts. People with low income sometimes need support to make ends meet. We maintain this support to provide basic security.

121112.explanation: This kind of help prevents bigger problems. We want to avoid more people falling through the cracks. This requires humanity and trust in residents. Spending less money on this now will lead to higher costs later.

121115.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but a lower speed limit is not suitable everywhere. Each street or area should be assessed for what is necessary and logical. For example, 30 km/h in residential neighborhoods, but not on through roads. This allows room for tailored solutions while keeping roads safe and accessible.

121116.explanation: Good care is about quality and availability. The municipality must ensure good care for everyone without distinction or making care unnecessarily complicated. An inclusive society also requires acceptance of other religions, cultures, and beliefs.

121117.explanation: We choose appropriate and timely support for children and families. In youth care, quality must come first. Too much focus on price can lead to poorer care.

121121.explanation: New industrial sites are not automatically necessary. We first make better use of existing sites and look at mixing living and working. This way, we preserve space for housing, nature, and livability.

121122.explanation: Pro Olst-Wijhe wants priority to be given to people from the municipality and people with a residence permit. This respects the housing needs of everyone. However, continuing to build more affordable homes is the real solution to the housing shortage.

121126.explanation: Our municipality has many waterways and other places with water. Swimming lessons are important for children's safety. By supporting swimming at school, we ensure every child learns to swim.

121127.explanation: Education is the responsibility of schools. They must primarily focus on developing basic skills. Attention to regional languages can be valuable but should not become mandatory. Schools decide how to implement this themselves.

121130.explanation: The housing crisis requires smart solutions. Renting out rooms can help create housing more quickly, especially for starters and singles. This is only possible if it is well regulated and does not cause nuisance. That calls for clear rules and good enforcement.

121131.explanation: We prefer smarter use of existing places and good alternatives, such as walking, cycling, and public transport. More parking spaces are not always the answer. Extra space for cars often comes at the expense of greenery and livability.

121133.explanation: The IJssel River is an important asset of our municipality. By better organizing it for recreation and tourism, we strengthen the local economy and keep the area attractive for residents and visitors.

121134.explanation: Historic buildings give our villages character. Preservation is important, also when building homes. We seek solutions where new construction is possible without losing the heritage and village appearance.

121081.explanation: A (advisory) referendum requires very clear communication and explanation, but it is a great way for residents to make their voices heard.

121085.explanation: The municipality supports local initiatives and helps find the right subsidies.

121088.explanation: This may create undesirable living situations, such as many people living in one house.

121091.explanation: That is up to the entrepreneur, although the municipality could approach the relevant entrepreneur to explore if more sustainable solutions can be devised.

121092.explanation: The municipality must facilitate a central fireworks show together with local stakeholders and organizers of New Year's Eve celebrations.

121099.explanation: It is a noble goal, but Olst-Wijhe’s financial resources are so tight that this is not or barely feasible.

121100.explanation: A municipal coordinator should examine if and how the association can continue to exist. Also, the local sports advisor from NOC*NSF can be consulted for advice.

121103.explanation: The top 5 annoyances, minor crime and undermining also occur in our municipality. More enforcement officers would allow better control here.

121104.explanation: Illegal dumping, vandalism, and theft might be prevented with (mobile) camera surveillance.

121108.explanation: The number of holiday parks is minimal; we do not expect our own residents to benefit from them soon.

121109.explanation: We want to build first within villages and centers. If that is no longer possible, then add a street at the edge of a village.

121110.explanation: It helps keep people involved in the local community.

121111.explanation: Residents should receive the help they need, and we cannot provide that if we push them (further) into debt.

121112.explanation: Olst-Wijhe has done this for years; in our municipality, the limit is set at 130% of the social assistance norm.

121115.explanation: Large parts of the built-up areas in Olst-Wijhe already have a speed limit of 30 km/h. This improves safety, especially for cyclists and pedestrians.

121116.explanation: Provided it fits within the existing care offer and financial possibilities.

121117.explanation: Quality had to be selected to prevent escalation to much higher costs.

121121.explanation: This has already been established in the industrial site vision; expansion is planned in Olst-Noord and Wesepe.

121122.explanation: (Former) residents of Olst-Wijhe have to wait a long time for housing, and starters sometimes have to live with their parents for years. There must be a good balance, and we must certainly offer opportunities to status holders.

121126.explanation: The idea is very sympathetic, but Olst-Wijhe does not have the finances for this. However, we believe this should be financed from The Hague.

121127.explanation: It is not up to the municipality to determine or influence the teaching materials.

121130.explanation: Olst-Wijhe is not a student city, and therefore it does not make sense to make this choice.

121131.explanation: There is plenty of building in the centers, and meeting parking needs is part of that. Additionally, car sharing, public transport, etc., can relieve parking pressure.

121133.explanation: There is a huge recreational opportunity with the unique IJssel River, which we must seize with both hands.

121134.explanation: Depending on the type of historic building, location, etc. If the historic building would block a nice extension, another solution must be found to preserve the history.

121081.explanation: We find it important that residents can give their opinion and be heard. However, we find a referendum too drastic a measure.

121085.explanation: We consider sustainable energy important. Many European and national subsidies and schemes already exist to support this.

121088.explanation: We find it most important that the quality of housing is maintained.

121091.explanation: We prefer infrared and electric heating because they are more environmentally friendly.

121099.explanation: Public funds should benefit society. Individual music lessons contribute little to this. Initiatives such as a school orchestra, Music Network Salland, and joint performances do contribute and we gladly support those.

121100.explanation: Sports clubs make an important social contribution and play a key role in social cohesion in the villages. They provide opportunities for meeting and stimulate physical activity.

121103.explanation: The focus should primarily be on undermining and high-impact crimes.

121104.explanation: Freedom and privacy are a great good.

121108.explanation: Yes, provided this can be done safely.

121110.explanation: Participation and meaning are important for people and society. Everyone should be able to take part.

121112.explanation: Initiatives like Financially in Control are important to help people out of debt and prevent poverty. It is important that no one falls through the cracks.

121115.explanation: Safety around schools and in neighborhoods is important. At the same time, traffic flow and driving comfort on main roads also remain important.

121117.explanation: Quality comes first, preferably in the immediate surroundings.

121122.explanation: Vulnerable people should be given priority.

121127.explanation: This is important, but the school itself decides on the curriculum.

121130.explanation: Living space must be utilized optimally and vacancies must be prevented.

121133.explanation: We mainly want to focus on walking paths. No permanent campsites or large-scale facilities, and with a focus on local residents.

121081.explanation: Many issues the municipality faces are too complicated to be addressed in a referendum. So: participation yes, referendum: no.

121085.explanation: Agree with the statement when it comes to initiating projects. Subsidies, as far as the VVD is concerned, are always temporary.

121088.explanation: This way, seasonal workers do not compete with local housing demand.

121091.explanation: Let's leave this to hospitality entrepreneurs themselves.

121092.explanation: As far as the VVD is concerned, this is not a task for the municipality.

121099.explanation: See question 18. What applies to sports largely also applies to music.

121100.explanation: There are conditions attached. If a club is no longer viable due to a small number of members, no subsidy/contribution will be given.

121103.explanation: Living in freedom means you must also feel safe. At home, in your neighborhood, and in the municipality. In recent years, the municipality has declined in terms of safety. The VVD wants to counter this by increasing police and enforcement officers.

121104.explanation: The VVD is not against camera surveillance but currently sees no suitable locations for installation. We trust in neighborliness and addressing each other regarding vandalism and/or undesirable behavior.

121108.explanation: According to the VVD, this is not the solution to the housing shortage.

121109.explanation: Not scattering sprinkles in rural areas, but adding a small street is our motto.

121110.explanation: Everyone has a talent. The VVD believes that you should then also use it for society.

121111.explanation: By forgiving debts, the poverty trap remains in place. People with low incomes also use the municipal facilities and services, which are (partly) funded by local taxes.

121112.explanation: This is how the so-called poverty trap is combated.

121115.explanation: We don't like that 'must' so much. If motorized and non-motorized traffic are well separated, the legal speed limit of 50 km/h is fine.

121116.explanation: In these times of labor shortages, people should be grateful to receive care at all. Care, according to the VVD, is not tied to culture, religion, or beliefs.

121117.explanation: Assuming that certified organizations also deliver (sufficient) quality.

121121.explanation: It is important that the municipality provides space for residents to live, work, and recreate. Companies provide not only employment but also governance, sponsorship, and livability.

121122.explanation: The VVD finds it hard to accept that local youth and residents have been on waiting lists for years while newcomers are prioritized.

121126.explanation: See answer regarding sports and music.

121127.explanation: The VVD finds it important to preserve the native language and culture. It is important that children know where they live and understand the customs, rituals, and (regional) culture.

121130.explanation: If this is done in a neat, meandering way, it makes a meaningful contribution to addressing the housing shortage.

121133.explanation: A great opportunity according to the VVD that puts the municipality well on the tourism map!

121134.explanation: Cherish the past and the (unique) village views. We see in Raalte what happens when you don’t do that…

statements.title: Historic buildings and village scenes in Olst-Wijhe must not be lost to new construction.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety should be employed in Olst-Wijhe.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The residents of Olst-Wijhe should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Olst-Wijhe moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV surveillance in Olst-Wijhe.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include instruction in the Sallands dialect.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in Olst-Wijhe.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The IJssel near Olst and Wijhe should be improved for tourism.
